name: ARIA required ID references exist
description: This rule checks that every ID reference required by WAI-ARIA exists
applicability_text:
This rule applies to any `aria-controls` attribute defined on an [HTML element][namespaced element] for which one of the following is true:

- <dfn>expanded combobox</dfn>: the element is a [semantic][] `combobox` with an `aria-expanded` [attribute value][] of `true`; or
- <dfn>scrollbar</dfn>: the element is a [semantic][] `scrollbar`.
expectations: Each test target's [attribute value][] is a space-separated list of one or more IDs. At least one of those IDs must match an `id` [attribute value][] in the same [shadow tree][] or, if not within a [shadow tree][], within the same [document][document tree].

examples.name: Passed Example 1
examples.description: The `aria-controls` [attribute value][] of this `scrollbar` matches the `id` of the `main` element in the same document.
examples.code: Lorem ipsum...

examples.name: Passed Example 2
examples.description: The `aria-controls` [attribute value][] of this expanded `combobox` matches the `id` of the `ul` element in the same document.
examples.code:
• Zebra
  • Zoom

examples.name: Passed Example 3
examples.description: The `aria-controls` [attribute value][] of this `scrollbar` has two IDs. The `content-2` ID matches the `id` of the `main` element in the same document.
examples.code: Lorem ipsum...

examples.name: Failed Example 1
examples.description: The `aria-controls` attribute of this expanded `combobox` references an ID of `popup_listbox` which does not exist in the document.

examples.name: Failed Example 2
examples.description: The `aria-controls` attribute of this `scrollbar` references IDs of `content-1` and `content-2`. Neither of these IDs exist in the document.
examples.code: Lorem ipsum...

examples.name: Failed Example 3
examples.description: The `aria-controls` attribute of this expanded `combobox` references a `popup_listbox` ID. This `id` exists, but in a different DOM tree as the `combobox`.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 1
examples.description: The `aria-controls` attribute is defined on a `combobox` which does not have an `aria-expanded` [attribute value][] of `true`.
examples.code:
<label for="tag_combo">Tag</label>
<input type="text" id="tag_combo" role="combobox" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="popup_listbox" />

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 2
examples.description: This `aria-controls` attribute is not defined on a [semantic][] `scrollbar` nor `combobox`.

background: This rule is written specifically for `aria-controls`, because it is the only [ID Reference List][] property that is [required by WAI-ARIA][]. The `aria-controls` property is only required by the `scrollbar` role and by an expanded `combobox`. There are no [ID Reference][] properties that are required by WAI-ARIA for any role.
accessibility_support: Some user agents treat the value of `aria-*` attribute as case-sensitive (even when these are not IDs) while some treat them as case-insensitive.
